---
title: "Circadian Phenotyping in Visual Impairment"
subtitle: "Data-driven circadian phenotyping using actigraphy"
description: "A data-driven framework for identifying circadian phenotypes in visually impaired participants using non-parametric actigraphy metrics, dimensionality reduction, clustering, and SHAP-based interpretation."
date: "2025-02-01"
image: ../images/actigraphy_clustering.svg
categories: [Research Pipeline, Actigraphy, Chronobiology, Python, Reproducible Research]
---

## About

This project develops a data-driven framework for identifying and characterising circadian phenotypes in visually impaired participants, who lack the primary light input that synchronises circadian rhythms in sighted individuals.

Non-parametric actigraphy metrics are used as features, with dimensionality reduction and clustering applied to identify phenotypic groups. SHAP values provide interpretable explanations of which actigraphy features drive each phenotype.

## Methods

- Non-parametric actigraphy metrics (IS, IV, RA, M10, L5, and more)
- Dimensionality reduction (UMAP)
- Unsupervised clustering
- SHAP-based feature interpretation
- Statistical comparison across phenotypic groups

## Links

- 💻 [GitHub](https://github.com/circadia-bio/nonparametric-actigraphy-clustering)
